Titulo: Solution Architect for SAP Sourcing and Procurement (m/f/d)
Descripcion_Completa:
Join LIEBHERR, a global leader in engineering and manufacturing, as we advance our digital transformation journey. We are looking for a motivated SAP S/4 HANA Solution Architect – Sourcing and Procurement (m/f/d) to play a key role in our newly established SAP Center in Madrid.


In this role, you will own and shape the Sourcing and Procurement domain of Liebherr’s global S/4HANA template for our Sales and Service companies (“Mixed Sales Companies”), supporting a multi-division environment including spanning Aerospace, Construction Machinery, Appliances, and other industrial segments, as well as business models such as remanufacturing and limited manufacturing.


You will drive the design of standardized, scalable, and efficient sourcing and procurement solutions, promoting SAP best practices and Clean Core principles, while ensuring compliance, transparency and process harmonization across entities. You will be part of an international, cross-functional team that values innovation, methodology and long-term thinking, making a direct impact on the way Liebherr runs its business worldwide.

Creating passion: your responsibilities
Own the end-to-end Sourcing and Procurement solution architecture within the global S/4HANA template for Sales and Service companies, ensuring consistency, scalability and compliance.
Design and govern global template solutions across core Sourcing and Procurement areas such as: Strategic sourcing and RFx, Operational procurement (PR, PO, contracts), Supplier management, Invoice verification (in integration with Finance), Service procurement, and Procurement analytics and reporting.
Translate complex business and statutory requirements from multiple countries and divisions into harmonized, standard SAP solutions minimizing custom development, driving process harmonization and simplification.
Lead and facilitate global design workshops with Procurement stakeholders and local entities.
Design and govern the integration of S/4HANA Procurement with Finance (AP, asset procurement, tax), Inventory and logistics processes, Manufacturing and remanufacturing scenarios, Service and spare parts processes, and Supplier collaboration and external platforms where relevant.
Act as a trusted advisor to business stakeholders, providing expert guidance across all project phases: Design, realization, testing, cutover, go-live and post go-live stabilization.
Contribute to change management activities, ensuring adoption of new processes and S/4HANA solutions.
Support the creation of solution documentation leveraging tools such as JIRA and Confluence.
Collaborate closely with an international, cross-functional team to deliver high quality, scalable solutions that support our global growth.
Travel (up to 30%) to Liebherr sites worldwide to engage directly with business teams and support deployments.
Contributing your strengths: your qualifications
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering, Information Technology, Business Administration, or related field.
7+ years of SAP experience in Sourcing and Procurement, including significant exposure to SAP S/4HANA Procurement.
Proven experience as a Solution Architect or Lead consultant in international SAP programs or template-based rollouts.
Strong understanding of procurement processes in Sales and Service-oriented businesses, ideally in industrial or equipment related industries.
Deep expertise in S/4HANA Procurement capabilities, including Purchasing and contract management, Supplier lifecycle processes, Release strategies and approvals, Service procurement and Integration with Finance and logistics.
Experience designing global templates across multiple countries and legal entities.
Demonstrated ability to analyze business challenges and translate them into SAP solutions that drive efficiency and value.
Familiar with JIRA and Confluence (or similar tools) for task management and team collaboration.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Fluency in English (written and spoken); additional languages (e.g., German) are an advantage.
A collaborative mindset and ability to work effectively in multicultural and cross-functional teams.
Willingness to travel internationally (up to 30%) as required by project work.
SAP S/4HANA Sourcing and Procurement certification is a strong plus.
Our commitment to you: your benefits

At Liebherr, we believe people are at the heart of our success. As part of our international team, you’ll enjoy a secure role in a family-owned company that values innovation, collaboration, and long-term career growth:




Competitive compensation and benefits package that recognizes your expertise
Flexible and hybrid working model
Creative freedom and responsibility to shape processes and solutions in our global transformation
Continuous learning and development with tailored training and certification opportunities
Meal vouchers
Life and accident insurance
Option to include a premium private health insurance package as part of the flexible remuneration
A safe, stable and international workplace within a trusted family business that invests in people


Please note that we can only accept applications submitted through our website, using the online application form.


We do not accept applications via recruitment agencies for this position.


Have we awoken your interest? Then we look forward to receiving your online application. If you have any questions, please contact Karoliina Rissanen.

Liebherr is a family-run technology company that is not only one of the largest construction machinery manufacturers in the world, but also offers high-quality, user-oriented products and services in many other areas. Titulo: Solution Architect for SAP Sales and Distribution (m/f/d)
Descripcion_Completa:
Join LIEBHERR, a global leader in engineering and manufacturing, as we advance our digital transformation journey. We are looking for a motivated SAP S/4 HANA Solution Architect – Sales & Distribution (m/f/d) to play a key role in our newly established SAP Center in Madrid.


In this role, you will own and shape the Sales and Distribution domain of Liebherr’s global S/4HANA template for our Sales and Service companies (“Mixed Sales Companies”), supporting a multi-division environment spanning Aerospace, Construction Machinery, Appliances, and other industrial segments, as well as business models such as remanufacturing and limited manufacturing.


You will drive the design of standardized, scalable, and customer-centric solutions, promoting SAP best practices and Clean Core principles, while ensuring that harmonized and efficient end-to-end order-to-cash processes across entities. You will be part of an international, cross-functional team that values innovation, methodology and long-term thinking, making a direct impact on the way Liebherr runs its business worldwide.

Creating passion: your responsibilities
Own the end-to-end Sales & Distribution solution architecture within the global S/4HANA template for Sales and Service companies, ensuring consistency, scalability and compliance.
Design and govern global template solutions across key Sales & Distribution areas such as: Order-to-cash processes, Quotation and contract management, Pricing and discount structures, Billing and credit management, Returns and complaints processing, Intercompany sales scenarios, Export and trade compliance where relevant.
Translate complex business and statutory requirements from multiple countries and divisions into harmonized, standard SAP solutions minimizing custom development, driving process harmonization and simplification.
Lead and facilitate global design workshops with sales, customer service, and local business stakeholders.
Design and govern the integration of S/4HANA SD with Finance (billing, revenue postings, credit), Service and after-sales processes, Procurement and logistics execution, Manufacturing and remanufacturing scenarios and CRM and Field Services processes in Microsoft Dynamics where financially relevant.
Act as a trusted advisor to business stakeholders, providing expert guidance across all project phases: Design, realization, testing, cutover, go-live and post go-live stabilization.
Contribute to change management activities, ensuring adoption of new processes and S/4HANA solutions.
Support the creation of solution documentation leveraging tools such as JIRA and Confluence.
Collaborate closely with an international, cross-functional team to deliver high quality, scalable solutions that support our global growth.
Travel (up to 30%) to Liebherr sites worldwide to engage directly with business teams and support deployments.
Contributing your strengths: your qualifications
Bachelor’s or master’s degree in Engineering, Information Technology, Business Administration, or related field.
7+ years of SAP experience in Sales & Distribution, including significant exposure to SAP S/4HANA SD.
Proven experience as a Solution Architect or Lead consultant in international SAP programs or template-based rollouts.
Strong understanding of sales and order-to-cash processes in Sales and Service-oriented businesses, ideally in industrial or equipment related industries.
Deep expertise in S/4HANA Sales & Distribution capabilities, including Order-to-cash processes, advanced pricing and conditions, billing and revenue integration, credit management, intercompany sales and integration with logistics and service processes.
Experience designing global templates across multiple countries and legal entities.
Demonstrated ability to analyze business challenges and translate them into SAP solutions that drive efficiency and value.
Familiar with JIRA and Confluence (or similar tools) for task management and team collaboration.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Fluency in English (written and spoken); additional languages (e.g., German) are an advantage.
A collaborative mindset and ability to work effectively in multicultural and cross-functional teams.
Willingness to travel internationally (up to 30%) as required by project work.
SAP S/4HANA Sales certification is a strong plus.
Our commitment to you: your benefits

At Liebherr, we believe people are at the heart of our success. As part of our international team, you’ll enjoy a secure role in a family-owned company that values innovation, collaboration, and long-term career growth:




Competitive compensation and benefits package that recognizes your expertise
Flexible and hybrid working model
Creative freedom and responsibility to shape processes and solutions in our global transformation
Continuous learning and development with tailored training and certification opportunities
Meal vouchers
Life and accident insurance
Option to include a premium private health insurance package as part of the flexible remuneration
A safe, stable and international workplace within a trusted family business that invests in people

Descripcion_Completa:
At Air Liquide Healthcare Iberia, we are specialists in the manufacture and comercialization of medical and healthcare gases. We manufacture and supply medical gases; we develop, install, maintain equipment and propose essential solutions for their proper use.



We work with the highest safety standards in all our processes to ensure the proper use and handling of our gases, services and equipment at all times. In addition, we have a large team of experts and professionals who have an in-depth knowledge of our customers' needs.






How will you CONTRIBUTE and GROW?



La misión principal de este puesto será responsabilizarse de la interacción con los clientes en el ámbito de las integraciones, al mismo tiempo que gestionar los proyectos recogiendo las interacciones de los clientes e interaccionar internamente con todos los stakeholders oportunos para gestionar la correcta implementación de las soluciones informáticas.
Por último, la misión pasará por garantizar la compliance de las aplicaciones con las reglas del grupo y los objetivos del negocio con la finalidad de eficiencia informática y monetaria.





Las principales funciones del puesto, entre otras:

Entender las necesidades del cliente y colaborar con clientes en el desarrollo de planes de trabajo que satisfagan las necesidades de ambas partes
Participar en la consultoría y soporte a los proyectos implicándose en el diseño, flujos de trabajo, plantillas y definición de mejores prácticas
Coordinar los proyectos de implementación de soluciones de integración en interno, con los proveedores y con los clientes
Responsabilizarse de la monitorización y seguimiento de las aplicaciones de su ámbito desarrollando los cuadros de mando que se acuerden
Realizar formaciones y soporte a usuarios, ya sea interno o externo, a través de los canales y periodicidad acordados
Supervisar la documentación técnica de los proyectos en cumplimiento de la calidad establecida
Participar activamente en la calidad de la solución técnica desde el diseño hasta la comprobación de su cumplimiento
Colaborar con el departamento IT para garantizar la viabilidad de la evolución a otros entornos técnicos y funcionales de forma progresiva
Analizar las propuesta de planes de acción para los defectos u errores que surjan
Colaborar en el cumplimiento del Sistema Integrado de Calidad, Medio Ambiente y PRL.
Cumplir las normas y políticas de calidad, IMS, IT y seguir las buenas prácticas

#LI-MC1





Are you a MATCH?





Serás la persona que encaje en esta posición si cumples los siguientes requisitos:

Estudios universitarios: titulación universitaria de grado medio-a nivel de diplomatura o ingeniería técnica/informática
Experiencia de, al menos, 5 años en posiciones similares
Conocimientos en Planificación/Gestión de Proyectos
Conocimentos en AWS
Conocimientos en EDI e intercambios de mensajería entre sistemas, Talend, XML, TIBCO, Sybase

Join LIEBHERR, a global leader in engineering and manufacturing, as we advance our digital transformation journey. We are looking for a motivated SAP S/4 HANA Solution Architect – Finance (m/f/d) to play a key role in our newly established SAP Center in Madrid.


In this role, you will own and shape the Finance domain of Liebherr’s global S/4HANA template for our Sales and Service companies (“Mixed Sales Companies”), supporting a multi-division environment spanning Aerospace, Construction Machinery, Appliances, and other industrial segments, as well as business models such as remanufacturing and limited manufacturing.


You will drive the design of standardized, scalable, and compliant finance solutions, promoting SAP best practices and Clean Core principles, while ensuring that local statutory and business requirements are met in a harmonized way. You will be part of an international, cross-functional team that values innovation, methodology and long-term thinking, making a direct impact on the way Liebherr runs its business worldwide.

Creating passion: your responsibilities
Own the end-to-end Finance solution architecture within the global S/4HANA template for Sales and Service companies, ensuring consistency, scalability and compliance.
Design and govern global template solutions across core Finance areas such as: General Ledger, Accounts Payable & Receivable, Asset Accounting, Tax and statutory reporting, Revenue recognition and service-related billing integration, Intercompany processes, and Financial closing and reporting.
Translate complex business and statutory requirements from multiple countries and divisions into harmonized, standard SAP solutions minimizing custom development, driving process harmonization and simplification.
Lead and facilitate global design workshops with Finance stakeholders and local entities.
Design and govern the integration of S/4HANA Finance with Sales and Distribution, Service Management, Procurement, Manufacturing and remanufacturing scenarios and CRM and Field Services processes in Microsoft Dynamics where financially relevant.
Act as a trusted advisor to business stakeholders, providing expert guidance across all project phases: Design, realization, testing, cutover, go-live and post go-live stabilization.
Contribute to change management activities, ensuring adoption of new processes and S/4HANA solutions.
Support the creation of solution documentation leveraging tools such as JIRA and Confluence.
Collaborate closely with an international, cross-functional team to deliver high quality, scalable solutions that support our global growth.
Travel (up to 30%) to Liebherr sites worldwide to engage directly with business teams and support deployments.
Contributing your strengths: your qualifications
Bachelor’s or master’s degree in Finance, Information Technology, Business Administration, or related field.
7+ years of SAP Finance experience, including significant exposure to SAP S/4HANA Finance.
Proven experience as a Solution Architect or Lead consultant in international SAP programs or template-based rollouts.
Strong understanding of finance processes in Sales and Service-oriented businesses, ideally in industrial or equipment related industries.
Deep expertise in S/4HANA Finance capabilities, including Universal Journal, new asset accounting, parallel accounting, tax configuration, financial closing processes and integration with logistics and service processes.
Experience designing global templates across multiple countries and legal entities.
Demonstrated ability to analyze business challenges and translate them into SAP solutions that drive efficiency and value.
Familiar with JIRA and Confluence (or similar tools) for task management and team collaboration.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Fluency in English (written and spoken); additional languages (e.g., German) are an advantage.
A collaborative mindset and ability to work effectively in multicultural and cross-functional teams.
Willingness to travel internationally (up to 30%) as required by project work.
SAP S/4HANA Finance certification is a strong plus.
Our commitment to you: your benefits

At Liebherr, we believe people are at the heart of our success. As part of our international team, you’ll enjoy a secure role in a family-owned company that values innovation, collaboration, and long-term career growth:

Competitive compensation and benefits package that recognizes your expertise
Flexible and hybrid working model
Creative freedom and responsibility to shape processes and solutions in our global transformation
Continuous learning and development with tailored training and certification opportunities
Meal vouchers
Life and accident insurance
Option to include a premium private health insurance package as part of the flexible remuneration
A safe, stable and international workplace within a trusted family business that invests in people

Prácticas en Estudios y Soluciones Eléctricas


Nuestro negocio de Redes de Electricidad España (UFD) está inmerso en un apasionante reto de transformación, que tiene como misión la digitalización y sensorización de nuestra red eléctrica para evolucionar de una red meramente física a una red virtual, donde el análisis de datos y la inteligencia artificial nos permitan gestionar y dar respuesta a todas las necesidades del nuevo ecosistema sostenible.
¡Queremos ser los mejores en lo que hacemos! Trabajando en equipo, haciendo más con menos y desarrollando a cada persona, para crecer juntos tanto en el ámbito de Redes Electricidad España como en el de otros negocios.
En este contexto ofrecemos una Beca en el negocio de Redes Electricidad España.


Estas prácticas te permitirán participar en:
Generación de maestros de reclamaciones y análisis de la red en función de los inputs de calidad de suministro:
Análisis de calidad del producto: Realizar estudios detallados sobre subtensiones y sobretensiones en la red de baja tensión, asociadas a reclamaciones de clientes de UFD. Al analizar estas reclamaciones, se ha observado que las soluciones aplicadas para resolver los problemas se pueden clasificar en distintos tipos. Para cada tipo se seleccionará una solución representativa, que estará respaldada por un análisis y una justificación detallada, explicando el motivo de la elección y cómo se aplicará en cada caso.
Representación y análisis de datos: Utilizar programas de gestión de datos para procesar y filtrar grandes volúmenes de datos obtenidos de los diferentes eventos registrados, aplicando los criterios aprendidos durante las prácticas y aquellos empleados en escenarios reales. Además, emplear Power BI como herramienta para la representación gráfica de las anomalías en la red, facilitando la interpretación de los resultados y la toma de decisiones de manera más práctica y eficiente.
Gestion y agrupación de datos: Elaborar el análisis e ingesta de datos de manera agrupada.

Ser estudiante del último curso de Grado o Máster en Ingeniería Eléctrica.
Conocimientos de redes eléctricas en baja y media tensión y los distintos elementos que componen el sistema eléctrico

Descripcion_Completa:
Locations: Madrid | Barcelona

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


We Are BCG X


We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions.


What You'll Do

Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions.
You'll be part of our rapidly growing team who will have a chance to apply data science methods and analytics to real-world business situations across a variety of industries to drive significant business impact. You'll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions.
Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X. Successful candidates are intellectually curious builders who are biased toward action, creative, and communicative.
We are looking for dedicated individuals with a passion for data science, statistics, operations research and redefining organizations into AI led innovative companies. Successful candidates possess the following:
Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams
Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations
Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner
Love building things and are comfortable working with modern development tools and writing code collaboratively (bonus points if you have a software development or DevOps experience)
Significant experience applying advanced analytics to business situations and a proven ability to synthesize complex data
Deep understanding of modern machine learning techniques and their mathematical underpinnings, and can translate this into business implications for our clients
Have strong project management skills


#BCGXjob


What You'll Bring

REQUIREMENTS:
Currently enrolled in a university master’s or PhD degree program in Computer Research Science, Data Science, Statistics, Operations Research, or related field.
You must be in your last academic year (Master Degree)
You need full-time availability for at least 8 weeks to have a complete immersion in a project
You need a fluent level of Spanish, both written and spoken, in order to serve our clients (also fluent English)


TECHNOLOGIES:
Programming Languages: Python

Titulo: AI Engineer, Internship, Spain - BCG X
Descripcion_Completa:
Locations: Madrid | Barcelona

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


We Are BCG X


We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions.


What You'll Do

Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions.
You’ll be part of our rapidly growing engineering team and help to build the next generation of AI solutions. You’ll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions. Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X.
We are looking for dedicated individuals with a passion for software development, large-scale data analytics and redefining organizations into AI led innovative companies. Successful candidates possess the following:
Apply software development practices and standards to develop robust and maintainable software
Actively involved in every part of the software development life cycle
Experienced at guiding non-technical teams and consultants in and best practices for robust software development
Optimize and enhance computational efficiency of algorithms and software design
Motivated by a fast-paced, service-oriented environment and interacting directly with clients on new features for future product releases
Enjoy collaborating in teams to share software design and solution ideas
A natural problem-solver and intellectually curious across a breadth of industries and topics

#BCGXjob


What You'll Bring

REQUIREMENTS:
Currently enrolled in a university master’s or PhD degree program in Computer Research Science, Data Science, Statistics, Operations Research, or related field
TECHNOLOGIES:
Programming Languages: Python


#LI-DNI


#BCGXjob

Titulo: Senior Analyst - BCG Vantage - Energy - Power & Gas Retail
Descripcion_Completa:
Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


What You'll Do

As a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage on our Topic Activation path within BCG's cross topic Energy Practice Area you will work in a growing global team, providing industry expertise and insights whilst working together with case and proposal teams to provide knowledge assets, analysis and expert advisory. You’ll serve as a thought partner and content expert to case teams and topic leaders, helping structure and solve complex issues that will range significantly as per our client needs and latest developments and trends.

You will support commercialization efforts for the topic working, in conjunction with business leaders, through contributing analysis and insights to proposals, client workshops and marketing materials.


Additionally, as a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage, you will contribute to developing intellectual property & assets for the business and assist in onboarding and training junior colleagues based on your topic expertise.


Most of your time will be spent supporting case teams and developing Energy related content, working side by side with members of different backgrounds. You will dedicate time to further develop and deepen BCG’s expertise in these areas, and to support the Energy Practice, BCG Directors and Partners in business development initiatives.


BCG's Energy Practice Area help companies navigate an increasingly complex business climate.


In Energy we cover the Oil and Power & Gas sectors, including all "Green Energy" and Social Impact, Climate and Sustainability sectors. We work with the full range of players in the industry: integrated international oil companies (IOCs), national oil companies (NOCs), upstream players, oilfield service companies, refiners, petrochemical producers, and oil trading firms, integrated power and gas utilities, international power producers, renewable specialists, energy wholesalers and retailers, commodity traders, and infrastructure and industry service providers, water utilities and waste-management firms, governments, regulators, industry associations, NGOs, technology companies, OEMs and suppliers to the energy and environment industry, energy-intense heavy industry players.


YOU'RE GOOD AT

Solving client problems through formulating relevant research and/or analytical approaches in power and gas retail including and new products and services opportunity for utilities. Advanced excel modelling skills and high quantitative abilities are critical in this job position
Applying topic expertise when contributing to building and deploying a product/tool/data, taking different client contexts into account
Codifying knowledge and maintaining assets and tools
Working closely with topic leadership in defining business agenda
Being a self-starter, a proactive problem-solver who thrives in environments with minimal guidance and takes ownership of scoping and driving work forward. Intrapreneurial mindset highly valued
Communicating with case teams and stakeholders, in a credible and confident way
Working collaboratively and effectively in a group dynamically, often virtual, proficient in agile ways of working
Being flexible and bringing a curious and creative mindset, open to new things and able to propose innovative ideas
Navigating complexity and ambiguity
High critical thinking and being able to easily sense check with back of the envelope and quality checks analysis the quality of our messaging and outputs

RESPONSIBILITIES


Client Delivery

Leverages knowledge/experience to partner with consulting teams to identify and address critical knowledge needs e.g., what talent/skills are needed to drive the energy transition
Provides customized knowledge for client work, prepares accurate, well-developed analysis, in Office based formats
Delivers original analysis and insights to consulting team, provides thought partnership
Liaises with Research team to ensure that requests for expertise are addressed by Vantage and appropriate research is delivered

Topic and Practice Development

With consultants, collects, synthesizes, and leverages case team learning & inputs into new knowledge products (vignettes, experience)
Builds & updates new topic/sector-related customized knowledge products and tools
Controls Knowledge Management databases consistency
Reviews knowledge products & ensures they are current & relevant and updates when appropriate
Writes abstracts & indexes projects & PA documents
Collaborates on practice area intellectual capital development
Contributes to content development around topic/sector for PA meetings & trainings


What You'll Bring

2+ years of consulting in power and gas retail and/or power flexibility/demand response topics; candidates with consulting experience strongly preferred
In lieu of consulting experience, 3+ years minimum industry experience required; 4-6+ years of industry experience strongly preferred
Bachelor's Degree required (advanced degree preferred)
Fluency in English
Strong business acumen and problem-solving capabilities
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Outstanding interpersonal and communication skills to interact with internal and external stakeholder while working in a global collaborative team environment


Who You'll Work With

As a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage on our Topic Activation path, you will collaborate with global topic leads and colleagues across BCG to bring expertise and capabilities to the service of our internal and external clients. Our team is made up of a diverse pool of BCG Vantage positions that allow us to pursue exciting, innovative BCG Vantage careers.


Additional info

BCG Vantage is a powerhouse of expertise, seamlessly embedded within Practice Areas and markets to drive extraordinary impact. It equips BCG case teams and clients with cutting-edge expertise, proprietary assets, actionable data, and transformative insights that empower our case teams and enable our clients to achieve their aspirations.


With global experts across 20 Practices, covering more than 100 topics, sectors and offers, plus four focused geographic markets, BCG Vantage delivers unparalleled depth and breadth of insight & expertise. BCG Vantage consists of three career paths, each playing a crucial role in delivering value to clients, case teams and practice teams.


Client Focus (Embedded Expertise)

Integrates into client-facing teams to deliver direct client impact through expertise, know-how, and proprietary tools and assets


Topic Activation (Sector Expertise with Proprietary Tool & Data Solutions)

Combines deep domain insights with our know-how and proprietary assets to address targeted challenges directly with clients and project teams; co-creates insights on industry trends and functional topics, proprietary data and tools, and teams to codify IP that all enables BCG to deliver excellent business value with its clients


Research (Practice Topic and Sector-Aligned Research and Insights)

Delivers actionable research and insights tailored to the client project’s needs; works with qualitative and quantitative data, conducts research and provides synthesized results from sources including commercial data, public and open source, company and industry research, macro-economic, commodities and trade data.

Hey there! Let us introduce ourselves!

We're Doofinder, we were born to make technology accessible for companies of any size, anywhere, to grow. Since 2011, our team has developed a top search technology for websites and eCommerce, that helps online retailers increase their sales.

We are agile, open, inclusive, and customer-centric. But above it all, in Doofinder people come first. We are a company with people of many different nationalities, cultures, and languages. We sell all over the world and speak the language of our customers. That makes our environment a place where we learn every day: we are enriched by the origin, culture, and personal history of very different people who have a common dream – to make powerful, affordable products and to contribute to the sustainable future of our thousands of customers.

We are looking for a self-motivated and dedicated Account Executive. You will be part of a smart, innovative, and experienced team with a broad range of skills and a really sociable and positive attitude.


Key Responsibilities

You will play an important role in driving our business growth by acquiring new clients. You will contact potential clients, understanding their needs, and offering tailored solutions to meet their objectives via calls and emails. Your responsibilities will include:

Develop and execute strategies to acquire new clients.

Sales and Revenue Generation:

Develop and present compelling proposals and presentations to prospects and clients.

Negotiate contracts, pricing, and terms of agreements to close deals effectively.

Continuously monitor sales pipelines, track progress, and provide accurate forecasts. Meet or exceed sales targets and revenue objectives set by the company.


Required Skills and Experience

1-2 years of experience in outbound B2B sales, with a focus on SMBs.
Proven track record of managing the full sales cycle from prospecting to closing.
Ability to meet and exceed sales KPIs (quotas, conversion rates, lead generation targets).
Strong negotiation, presentation, and communication skills, with a consultative approach to selling.
Experience with CRM tools (e.g. HubSpot, SalesForce) and sales automation software.
Familiarity with inbound sales methodologies or content-driven sales techniques.

EU work permit is a must


Nice to Have:

Experience within the SaaS industry, particularly in e-commerce, search, or digital marketing solutions.
Has worked in fast-growing tech startups or high-growth environments.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to track and report on key sales metrics and performance indicators.
Familiarity with search technologies (e.g., Doofinder) or digital retail solutions.
Expertise in market research and competitor analysis.

Languages

Native Polish
Fluent in either English or Spanish

Real stability with a permanent contract (Indefinido) in an innovative and rapid growth company with a future project on e-commerce industry.

Join LIEBHERR, a global leader in engineering and manufacturing, as we advance our digital transformation journey. We are looking for a motivated SAP S/4 HANA Solution Architect – Manufacturing (m/f/d) to play a key role in our newly established SAP Center in Madrid.


In this role, you will own and shape the Manufacturing domain of Liebherr’s global S/4HANA template for our Sales and Service companies (“Mixed Sales Companies”), supporting a multi-division environment spanning Aerospace, Construction Machinery, Appliances, and other industrial segments, as well as business models such as remanufacturing and limited manufacturing.


You will drive the design of standardized, scalable, and efficient manufacturing solutions, promoting SAP best practices and Clean Core principles, while enabling flexible production and remanufacturing scenarios. You will be part of an international, cross-functional team that values innovation, methodology and long-term thinking, making a direct impact on the way Liebherr runs its business worldwide.

Creating passion: your responsibilities
Own the end-to-end Manufacturing solution architecture within the global S/4HANA template for Sales and Service companies, ensuring consistency, scalability and governance.
Design and govern global template solutions across key Manufacturing areas such as: Production planning and control, Shopfloor execution, Bill of materials and routings, MRP and capacity planning, Subcontracting and external processing, Remanufacturing and refurbishment processes, Limited/local assembly scenarios y Sales and Service entities.
Translate complex business and statutory requirements from multiple countries and divisions into harmonized, standard SAP solutions minimizing custom development, driving process harmonization and simplification.
Lead and facilitate global design workshops with manufacturing, operations and supply chain stakeholders.
Design and govern the integration of Manufacturing with Procurement and sourcing, Inventory and logistics, Quality management, Controlling and product costing, Service and spare parts processes, Engineering and PLM systems.
Act as a trusted advisor to business stakeholders, providing expert guidance across all project phases: Design, realization, testing, cutover, go-live and post go-live stabilization.
Contribute to change management activities, ensuring adoption of new processes and S/4HANA solutions.
Support the creation of solution documentation leveraging tools such as JIRA and Confluence.
Collaborate closely with an international, cross-functional team to deliver high quality, scalable solutions that support our global growth.
Travel (up to 30%) to Liebherr sites worldwide to engage directly with business teams and support deployments.
Contributing your strengths: your qualifications
Bachelor’s or master’s degree in Engineering, Operations Management, Information Technology, Business Administration, or related field.
7+ years of SAP experience in Manufacturing or Production Planning, including significant exposure to SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing (PP).
Proven experience as a Solution Architect or Lead consultant in international SAP programs or template-based rollouts.
Strong understanding of manufacturing and operations processes in industrial or equipment related industries. Experience in engineer-to-order or configure-to-order environments is a strong advantage.
Deep expertise in S/4HANA Manufacturing capabilities, including Production planning and execution, MRP and demand-driven planning, BOM and routing management, Subcontracting and external processing, Integration with quality and logistics, and Production costing integration with CO.
Experience designing global templates across multiple countries and legal entities.
Demonstrated ability to analyze business challenges and translate them into SAP solutions that drive efficiency and value.
Familiar with JIRA and Confluence (or similar tools) for task management and team collaboration.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Fluency in English (written and spoken); additional languages (e.g., German) are an advantage.
A collaborative mindset and ability to work effectively in multicultural and cross-functional teams.
Willingness to travel internationally (up to 30%) as required by project work.
SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing certification is a strong plus.
Our commitment to you: your benefits

At Liebherr, we believe people are at the heart of our success. As part of our international team, you’ll enjoy a secure role in a family-owned company that values innovation, collaboration, and long-term career growth:




Competitive compensation and benefits package that recognizes your expertise

Continuous learning and development with tailored training and certification opportunities

Work flexibility, where TomTom’ers, in agreement with their manager and team, use both the office and home to focus, collaborate, learn and socialize. At TomTom...

Titulo: Analyst - BCG Vantage, Procurement & Cost Excellence
Descripcion_Completa:
Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


What You'll Do

As an Analyst (A) within BCG’s Operations Practice Area, you will work in a growing global team. You will work together with case teams to provide knowledge assets, analysis and expert advisory for diagnostics and live projects and deliver against commercial priorities for the Operations Vantage Team business.

In addition, you will support with guidance from senior colleagues, your topic in developing intellectual property & knowledge assets and managing content on internal BCG websites, ensuring availability of latest, high-quality content. You will also support business development and go-to-market efforts in the procurement topic business, as opportunities arise, contributing research and analysis to marketing efforts.

The Operations Practice Area is one of BCG's functional practices and helps our clients create competitive advantage by enhancing their performance and building strategic capabilities. The Procurement Topic Area is an expert network of Vantage members and experienced consultants who help clients on specific cases related to the functions and disciplines within the topic (e.g., procurement strategy, operating model, digital transformation, sustainable procurement, risk & resilience, etc.).


As an Analyst on the Client Focus (CF) track for the Operations Practice Area & Procurement business, you will be:

Driving problem solving and solutioning for clients generating insights towards pragmatic solutions tailored to the business context
Leading development and commercialization of Procurement knowledge and assets, aiming to innovate our offers and enhance our go to market approaches

YOU'RE GOOD AT

Knowledgeable in Operations processes related to Procurement strategy, value delivery, operating model, cost optimization and Digital/Gen AI
Engaging and communicating with senior stakeholders, demonstrating expert presence and credibility
Strong work ethics, service-mentality and willingness to showcase expertise
Ability to multi-task & operate effectively in a matrix organization
Collaborative, team player – including in an international, virtual communications environment
Flexibility and bring an entrepreneurial mindset, open for new things and able to propose innovative ideas
Working in an ever changing and complex environment, and comfortable with ambiguity and navigating a fast-paced environment


What You'll Bring

2+ years of consulting experience in operations/procurement/cost optimization required; candidates with consulting experience strongly preferred
1+ years of Industry or specific sector experience
In lieu of consulting experience, 1-2+ years minimum experience in Procurement, FP&A, Finance roles required; 3-4+ years of experience strongly preferred
Bachelor/Master degree from a reputed university with demonstrated academic achievements
Proficiency with MS-Office Suite (especially Advanced Excel and Power Point). Additional programming skills are a plus. (Python)
Good communication skills to manage senior stakeholders’ expectations.
Proficiency in English; additional languages are a plus (Spanish/German/ French).

As an Analyst, you will collaborate with case teams and colleagues across BCG to bring expertise and capabilities to the service of our internal and external clients. Our team is made up of a diverse pool of Vantage positions that allow us to pursue exciting, innovative careers.


Additional info

The Vantage Team is a group of functional and/or industry experts leveraging deep domain knowledge to enable insight for case teams and clients. Vantage members are affiliated to BCG Practice Areas, plugged into the strategic agenda and building and deploying leading edge IP data tools/assets to deliver insight.

BCG's Vantage members are staffed on cases and help co-create insights with case teams and clients through expert perspectives on industry trends, delivery of proprietary data or tools in a particular function and complex research and analysis of market information. Vantage members also support the PA’s business development and proposals and help to organize and codify the knowledge that enables BCG to deliver superior business value for its clients.

Job Description:

The jobholder will be responsible for:

Acting as the main point of contact for quality matters for the relevant project(s) throughout the assembly, integration, and test (AIT) phases, including interfacing with other departments such as Manufacturing Engineering, Materials and Processes, AIT management etc.
Performing and coordinating inspections (incoming and outgoing inspections, KIPs, MIPs).
Managing non-conformities and deviations raised during the AIT phases, including organizing, conducting, and documenting internal Non-Conformity Review Board (NRB) meetings.
Preparing quality assurance documentation such as the Quality Assurance Plan, Quality Control Plan, inspection reports, etc., and contributing to other project documents such as the KIP/MIP Plan, Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA), Action Plan (PA), etc.
Traveling to support test campaigns from the QA perspective (frequently abroad).
Identifying and implementing measures to reduce the cost of quality (such as reducing inspections and sampling).
Initiating and leading the Root Cause Analysis process for critical and repetitive issues in the area.
Organizing and managing reviews such as Integration Readiness Reviews (IRRs).
Promoting a preventive quality mindset within the AIT team, ensuring the implementation of basic quality processes (quality authorization, quality delegation, operational surveillance, etc.).

REQUIRED SKILLS:

Engineering degree or equivalent (Physics, Chemistry, etc.).
Languages: fluent in Spanish (at least C1) and English (at least C1).
At least 5 years of experience in the quality assurance domain for space sector projects.
Experience working in a cleanroom and with hardware: performing inspections, monitoring integration activities, etc.
Familiarity with ECSS standards.
Good stakeholder management skills.

This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth.

We are expanding our Global Corporate Information Security (CIS) team and are looking for an Information Security Compliance Product Owner (m/f/d) to establish and scale our global security compliance and certification capabilities.


The Information Security Compliance Product Owner owns the Information Security Compliance Product within the Information Security Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) portfolio and is accountable for ensuring that regulatory, contractual, and certification requirements related to information and cybersecurity are identified, assessed, and integrated into the organization’s Information Security Framework (ISF).


This role combines product ownership, project delivery and service execution, working closely with internal and external stakeholders.

Creating passion: your responsibilities
Compliance Product Ownership & ISF Alignment: Define and own the Compliance Product scope, roadmap, operating model, and KPIs aligned with CIS and GRC strategy. Ensure continuous alignment of ISF components (policies, standards, procedures, control baselines) with regulatory, contractual, and certification requirements.


Regulatory Compliance: Maintain a centralized inventory of applicable information and cybersecurity regulations (e.g. NIS2, GDPR, CRA, EU AI Act, defense-related obligations). Perform regulatory applicability assessments and structured compliance gap analyses. Define, track, and report remediation plans for identified compliance gaps. Monitor regulatory changes and ensure timely updates to the ISF.


Security standards compliance and certification (ISO/IEC 27001): Govern ISMS and CSMS documentation, readiness, and support in companies certification activities, including maintaining required evidence and ensure delivery during internal and external audits, Track audit findings and corrective actions to closure for areas of responsibility.


Customer & Stakeholder Assurance: Support with answering to compliance and security assessments from customers, contract security clause reviews, and customer audits. Titulo: Senior Analyst - BCG Vantage, Airline Network Strategy & Planning
Descripcion_Completa:
Locations: London | Madrid | Paris | Frankfurt

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


What You'll Do

As a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage focused on Airline Network Strategy & Planning, you will serve as a go-to subject matter expert on airline network design, partnering closely with BCG case teams and industry leaders to shape analytically rigorous, commercially grounded recommendations.


You will act as a thought partner to case teams and sector/topic leaders, supporting proposal efforts, client engagements, IP development, and tool design. In this role, you will:

Be BCG's internal go-to expert on airline network strategy, helping case teams understand how airline networks really work and how to make sound network decisions
Spot gaps in BCG’s airline network knowledge, tools, and materials, and take ownership of filling those gaps by creating new analyses, frameworks, tools, and slide decks
Design and evaluate airline network strategies, such as which routes to fly, where to grow or shrink capacity, how networks should evolve over time, and how airlines should respond to demand, cost, and competitive changes
Work with consulting teams to turn network mechanics into clear strategy, making sure recommendations are practical, economically sound, and grounded in real airline constraints
Build and improve reusable network planning tools, including models and GenAI-enabled solutions that case teams can apply across multiple client engagements
Create executive-ready materials (slides, benchmarks, playbooks) that explain network strategy clearly for senior airline and consulting leaders
Join airline client meetings, working sessions, and calls to provide network strategy expertise and support ongoing analyses


YOU'RE GOOD AT

Structuring and solving complex airline network planning problems, particularly those involving tradeoffs between connectivity, utilization, profitability, and operational constraints
Translating detailed schedule- and network-level analyses into clear strategic insights for senior stakeholders
Working hands-on with data, models, and planning tools rather than purely conceptual analyses
Applying strong commercial judgment to ambiguous planning decisions with real financial impact
Collaborating across global teams in fast-paced, high-expectation environments


What You'll Bring

3–6+ years of direct airline network planning experience, such as:
Network planning or network strategy roles at a commercial airline, with exposure to route portfolio design, capacity allocation, and network evolution decisions
OR top-tier consulting experience with substantial hands-on exposure to airline network planning topics
Demonstrated experience with:
O&D demand analysis and route-level profitability
Schedule design and seasonal planning cycles
Route and frequency planning
Fleet and aircraft assignment tradeoffs
Hands-on experience with airline planning tools such as NetLine/Plan, Sabre AirVision, Amadeus Sky Suite, or similar platforms (direct usage strongly preferred)
Strong understanding of airline economics, network effects, scheduling constraints, and capacity planning logic (Experience primarily focused on crew planning or rostering is less relevant for this role.)
Bachelor’s degree required; Master’s degree preferred (e.g., in Engineering, Operations Research, Data Science, or Transportation Economics)
Interest in or experience applying advanced analytics and GenAI to network optimization and decision support
Comfort operating in a consulting environment, including owning slide creation and executive messaging for senior stakeholders based on detailed analytical work
Strong business judgment, analytical rigor, and interpersonal skills
Fluency in English; additional language skills are a plus


Who You'll Work With

As a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage on our Topic Activation path, you will collaborate with global topic leads and colleagues across BCG to bring expertise and capabilities to the service of our internal and external clients. Our team is made up of a diverse pool of BCG Vantage positions that allow us to pursue exciting, innovative BCG Vantage careers.


Additional info

BCG Vantage is a powerhouse of expertise, seamlessly embedded within Practice Areas and markets to drive extraordinary impact. It equips BCG case teams and clients with cutting-edge expertise, proprietary assets, actionable data, and transformative insights that empower our case teams and enable our clients to achieve their aspirations.

With global experts across 20 Practices, covering more than 100 topics, sectors and offers, plus four focused geographic markets, BCG Vantage delivers unparalleled depth and breadth of insight & expertise. BCG Vantage consists of three career paths, each playing a crucial role in delivering value to clients, case teams and practice teams.


Client Focus (Embedded Expertise)

Integrates into client-facing teams to deliver direct client impact through expertise, know-how, and proprietary tools and assets


Topic Activation (Sector Expertise with Proprietary Tool & Data Solutions)

Combines deep domain insights with our know-how and proprietary assets to address targeted challenges directly with clients and project teams; co-creates insights on industry trends and functional topics, proprietary data and tools, and teams to codify IP that all enables BCG to deliver excellent business value with its clients


Research (Practice Topic and Sector-Aligned Research and Insights)

Titulo: Airbus UpNext Spain - E2E Bench Manager (m/f)
Descripcion_Completa:
Job Description:

At Airbus UpNext, we fly future technologies at speeds well beyond what seems feasible today. We're looking for passionate individuals to join a new demonstrator project that is shaping the future of sustainable aviation by advancing the aircraft electrification roadmap.

This project will focus on the challenges and opportunities of High Voltage Electrical Systems , which are essential for next-generation, low-emission aircraft part of Airbus ambition to electrify the skies.

As part of a fast-paced, agile team, you will help create a unique, state-of-the-art test facility from the ground up. Your mission will be to explore novel HV technologies at their limits and beyond, generating invaluable data and learnings needed to build the robust and reliable aircraft of tomorrow by safely testing the unsafe.

If you have a burning desire to make an impact and want to work in a startup-like environment backed by the strength of Airbus, this is your opportunity to help us decarbonize the aerospace industry .

This position is based in Getafe ( Spain), and it comes with a limited contract linked to the deliverable of the demonstrator (estimated date end of April 2028)

Mission:

As the E2E Bench Manager, reporting directly to the Technical Director, you own the Industrial Test Means pillar. You will act as a technical leader and people manager, guiding a dedicated team of engineers to deliver. You are responsible for the conception, design, manufacturing, installation and commissioning of the complex industrial electrical and mechanical equipment that constitutes the test facility in time and quality. You will ensure the facility can generate, distribute and dissipate HV power - up to 1500 VDC - while safely containing destructive failure events.

Your Main Responsibilities:

Team Leadership: Lead a high-performing team of 2-3 UpNext Test Means Engineers. You will define objectives, manage their workload and foster a “safety-first” culture within your squad.

Define the Test Bench Needs : Based on System Architectures provided by System Design Office and Test Intentions given by the E2E Test Manager, specify needed test bench requirements.

Test Means Development : Detailing the electrical and mechanical architecture and design of the bench. Lead the manufacturing and build of the facility core test capabilities including the electrical industrial equipment and the supporting systems linked to it and the test functions required, like industrial cooling, safe containers, environmental chambers, mechanical installation, UUT power wiring & signal cabling, Test Support System control / acquisition interconnection, etc.

Electrical Safety & Operations : Perform risk analysis and establish all required documentation for human safety related to your perimeter. Define the operational procedures for the test bench. Ensure compliance with electrical safety standards and industrial regulations. You will act as the primary authority for procedures and safety during test means commissioning. Oversee the physical integration of the Test Cells, ensuring the proper integration between the building infrastructure and the test equipment.

Supplier Identification & Steering : Study the market selecting potential subcontractors. Perform Make / Buy trade-offs. Support call for tenders and negotiate technical proposals. Manage technical performance of external and internal suppliers delivering heavy electrical equipment and custom test rigs, ensuring adherence to Time, Cost, Quality objectives up to on-site acceptance test and in-service support activities.

In-Service Continuous Operability : Ensure by continuous improvements a high test means operational reliability once the Entry Into Service milestone is achieved. Plan, define and perform required maintenance tasks and define obsolescence management.

Risks & Opportunities : Identify and analyze risks & opportunities affecting E2E bench performance and/or capacity, implementing corrective and preventive actions agreed within stop/go reviews.





The responsibilities and the technical scope of the job might evolve according to the agile working environment of the project, so being flexible is important.

Your boarding pass

What you bring:

Master Degree in Electrical Engineering, Industrial Engineering or related field. 10+ years of experience in the design, development integration and operation of complex Electrical Test Means or other relevant positions. Valid electrical safety habilitations or eligibility to obtain them under Spanish regulations.

Expertise in Test Means architectures, Power Electronics and Electrical Distribution. Knowledge in thermal management systems.

Proven experience leading small technical teams or functional groups. You know how to delegate effectively while remaining hands-on to solve complex technical problems.

Knowledge of R&T Electrical systems would be a very valuable asset. Passion about technology and science.

Professional network including industrial players, research institutions and consultants.

Language: English at negotiation level. Spanish would be an advantage since it is valued for managing local interfaces and regulations.

Eagerness to take initiatives to propose simpler and efficient ways of working. Impatience and burning desire to attain rapidly, along with a healthy skepticism for progress. Ability to cope with short deadlines.

Values of transparency, humility & empathy. Autonomy, adaptability, availability & accountability . Readiness to step out of your comfort zone. Willingness to have fun.





What you get:


Pivotal Role in a Strategic Mission: play a key part in a project at the forefront of aircraft electrification contributing to Airbus’s decarbonization roadmap.


Build Something that last: Your work will create a high-value asset
and learning contribution for Airbus’s future.

Develop Critical Competences: Acquire and develop a sound background in High Voltage systems and technologies, a field essential for the future of aerospace. This experience can provide a unique career development opportunity within Airbus.

Work on the Edge of Technology: Explore disruptive technologies at their limits, deepening your knowledge of complex phenomena like arc-flash events and the effects of high-altitude conditions on electrical systems.

Agile and Empowering Environment: Thrive in a work culture that empowers you to act and think like a start-up while being supported by the strong expertise of Airbus.
We value an eagerness to take initiative and propose efficient ways of working.

A Great Team and Culture: Be part of a high-achieving, experienced and fun team in a shared open-space office with highly skilled colleagues. We are guided by the “Care for Each Other” value.

Flexibility & Fun: Enjoy the ability to craft your calendar with flexible hours and the opportunity to work remotely, alongside regular UpNext community bonding events. And yes, we have complimentary freshly ground coffe and beverages





The responsibilities and the technical scope of the job might evolve according to the agile working environment of the project, so being flexible is important.

About us

Founded in 2017, Airbus UpNext focuses on flying future technologies at speeds well beyond what seems feasible today. In doing so, it is actively shaping the future of the aerospace industry.

Designed as a talent incubator, Airbus UpNext is a place where you can act and think like a startup while building on the strong expertise of Airbus, a place where you are empowered to test & challenge new processes and ways of working.

Airbus UpNext’s demonstrators evaluate, mature and validate potential new products and services that encompass radical technological breakthroughs. It has three flagships locations in France, Germany and Spain, to rapidly develop and test future technologies in scaled applications .

Our mission is:



To identify the trends that could disrupt the future of the aerospace industry and evaluate them to demonstrate their potential as a viable product.

To accelerate traditional research cycles and prototype development to achieve proof of concept in a real environment and at a convincing scale and speed.

To develop real-world applications for emerging aerospace technologies, thereby driving their maturity and increasing their value.

To be THE reference for Technology Value Assessment





Our Values: #Keep it Simple #Be Audacious #Exploring Together

Our DNA: #Speed of Execution, #Caring for Each Other, #Open to the World

Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions.


What You'll Do

BCG X is BCG’s premier data science and advanced analytics capability with 1000+ data scientists, engineers, and analysts globally. Within BCG X, our X.Delivery Teams are our hybrid client-facing/consulting-support that leverages available tools and code bases to deliver powerful and specialized insights that help our clients tackle their most pressing business problems. We partner with BCG case teams across the full analytics value chain: framing new business challenges and problems, designing and executing innovative analytical solutions, and interpreting findings for our clients. GeoAnalytics is a sub-team in X.Delivery with expertise in geospatial data, analytics and technology. Our 35+ member team is a global resource, working with clients in every BCG region and in every industry area. It is a core member of BCG X - a rapidly growing analytics enterprise at BCG deploying a constellation of teams focused on driving practical results for BCG clients by applying leading edge technologies.


As an Analyst in GeoAnalytics, you will be solving a variety of location-based analytical problems for our clients. You will utilize your knowledge of geospatial data and analytics while using tools such as Geographic Information Systems (GIS), Python, and visualization/analytical platforms (ex. Tableau, Alteryx). You will leverage a range of techniques such as geospatial clustering, network analysis, location-based optimization and predictive analytics/modeling. You will be expected to distill insights from your analysis and link them to business objectives. You will contribute to IP development, content development, and support internal marketing.


You will also have opportunities to experiment with emerging capabilities, including the selective use of GenAI to enhance analysis workflows and accelerate insight generation.


What You'll Bring

Education & Experience Qualifications:

1-3 years of relevant work experience in GeoAnalytics with advanced degree preferred
Relevant degree in geospatial related field such as: Geography, Geology, Environmental Science, Remote Sensing, Urban Planning, Statistics/GeoStatistics, Applied Mathematics, or Computer Science
Previous experience working in a global organization or professional services firm is preferred


Demonstrated experience in the following:

Conducting web-based research for open data sources; handling, understanding, and assessing new datasets; and stitching together disparate datasets to conduct custom analyses
Applying geospatial data, tools, and technology to solve problems for businesses across multiple topics
Technical experience in the following:
GIS software such as ESRI ArcGIS or QGIS
Data management skills (e.g. data modeling, QA/QC, querying, analysis)
Data science programming/coding in Python and/or R including use of spatial libraries/packages (eg GeoPandas, Rasterio, GDAL/OGR, Terra..)
Business Intelligence/data visualization platforms such as Tableau, Power BI, etc.
Applied descriptive statistics and analytical methods (e.g., correlation, regression, clustering) within machine learning frameworks to address geospatial problems
Remote sensing skills (handling, processing, and deriving insights) and optimization skills (linear programming/heuristics) are beneficial
Web-GIS development skills (JavaScript, HTML, CSS) in ArcGIS environment (JS SDK, Python API, ExB custom widgets, etc.) are beneficial
Familiarity with optimization models applied to location-based problems (VRP, Network Optimization, Supply Chain Optimization, etc.) is beneficial

Join LIEBHERR, a global leader in engineering and manufacturing, as we advance our digital transformation journey. We are looking for a motivated Solution Architect – Microsoft Dynamics CRM (m/f/d) to play a key role in our newly established Digital Center in Madrid.


In this role, you will own and shape the global Microsoft Dynamics CRM template of Liebherr’s global S/4HANA template for our Sales and Service companies (“Mixed Sales Companies”).


You will drive the design of standardized, scalable, and customer-centric CRM solutions, promoting global harmonization, best practices, and a template-first approach across entities. You will be part of an international, cross-functional team that values innovation, methodology and long-term thinking, making a direct impact on how Liebherr engages with customers globally.

Creating passion: your responsibilities
Own the end-to-end Microsoft Dynamics CRM solution architecture within Liebherr’s global template, ensuring consistency, scalability and governance.
Design and govern global template solutions across CRM capabilities such as: Lead-to-opportunity and opportunity-to-order processes, Account and contact management, Customer lifecycle management, Installed base and asset-related customer views, Case and complaint management, Sales pipeline and activity management.
Translate complex business and statutory requirements from multiple countries and divisions into harmonized, standard CRM solutions minimizing custom development and ensuring a configuration-first approach.
Lead and facilitate global design workshops with sales, customer services, and after-sales stakeholders.
Design and govern the integration between Microsoft Dynamics CRM and SAP S/4HANA (SD, Service, Finance), Microsoft Dynamics Field Service, Installed base and asset data, and Marketing or digital platforms where relevant.

Contribute to change management activities, ensuring adoption of new processes and CRM solution.
Support the creation of solution documentation leveraging tools such as JIRA and Confluence.
Collaborate closely with an international, cross-functional team to deliver high quality, scalable solutions that support our global growth.
Travel (up to 30%) to Liebherr sites worldwide to engage directly with business teams and support deployments.
Contributing your strengths: your qualifications
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Marketing, Information Technology, Business Administration, or related field.
7+ years of experience in Microsoft Dynamics CRM / Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement, including large-scale implementations.
Proven experience as a Solution Architect or Lead consultant in Microsoft Dynamics CRM programs or template-based rollouts.
Strong understanding of B2B sales and service processes, ideally in industrial or equipment related industries.
Deep expertise in Dynamics CRM capabilities, including Sales and customer engagement modules, Case and Service Management, and Platform configuration and customization.
Experience designing and governing global CRM templates across multiple countries and legal entities. Experience integrating CRM platforms with ERP systems in complex B2B environments is a strong advantage.
Demonstrated ability to analyze business challenges and translate them into CRM solutions that drive efficiency and value.
Familiar with JIRA and Confluence (or similar tools) for task management and team collaboration.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Fluency in English (written and spoken); additional languages (e.g., German) are an advantage.

Solving problems with a forward-thinking mindset and suggesting workarounds
Developing judgement, starting to contribute beyond the PA, thinking analytically through alternatives

2-4+ years relevant experience
Bachelor’s degree
Demonstrated independence and autonomy in internal/external interactions
Ability to execute defined tasks that require independent judgment, based on processes/policies
Ability to effectively coordinate day today work; apply working knowledge of primary function and demonstrating the ability to solve problems within own role
Strong organizational and planning skills
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English
Ability to think analytically, adopting out the box thinking
Professional, service oriented, pro-active and flexible attitude
Ability to perform under pressure and demonstrate presence and maturity even in stressful situations
Proficiency in Outlook, PowerPoint, and Excel


Who You'll Work With

You will coordinate closely with Global and Regional PA Leaders, the Global Practice Management Director and the OPS Practice Are Management & Operations team, as well as the Business Services team, alongside Practice Area Coordinators both within the practice and across others.

Practice Area Management & Operations team members keep BCG’s industry and functional practice areas operating responsively and efficiently.
